title: clerodendrum tonkinense
intro: Clerodendrum tonkinense, also known as the tonkin glory bower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a climbing shrub native to Southeast Asia, commonly used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Clerodendrum tonkinense can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been traditionally used to treat a range of health benefits, including digestive issues and skin problems.
